Quality Education.reason: The course provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and teaches relevant technical GIS skills for employment (Target 4.4), as reflected in the reading "GIS Education...Programs".

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course teaches industry-standard GIS software, which can enhance skills for employment, but this is an indirect link to decent work and not a primary focus.
evidence: Industry-standard GIS software will be used in this course.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course teaches "Network Analysis" and methods to measure "Spatial Accessibility," which are key tools for planning and assessing infrastructure to ensure equitable access (Target 9.1).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course focuses on analyzing social data, including "Census Data," with readings on "equity and health care" and "Poverty," directly supporting the analysis of inequality (Target 10.2).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course uses spatial analysis to study governance, citing readings on "Public Service," "Political Risks," and "Spatial spillover effects of corruption," supporting Targets 16.5 and 16.6.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds capacity in analyzing spatially-referenced data like "Census Data," which is essential for monitoring SDGs and supports Target 17.18 on data disaggregation by geographic location.
evidence: Spatial data mining and geographic knowledge discovery
